title: Buildprint Mapper OS
creator: Agent Buildprint
summary: Agent-session workflow for mapping an existing repo into an executable, source-independent Buildprint packet with phase-flow replay, schema-valid runtime evidence, and fresh-agent proof gates.
plainDescription: Use Mapper OS when you already have a working repo and want an agent to turn it into a clean, reusable Buildprint: map the source, choose a scoped candidate, then extract a selected-buildprint packet that a fresh agent can implement through phase-flow, reviews, proof gates, and honest runtime evidence without reading the original code.
promise: A repo-to-Buildprint workflow that preserves scope fidelity, generates executable phase files, enforces runtime evidence honesty, requires architecture/UX/QA review contracts, and proves the packet with fresh-agent replay instead of trusting a static spec.
whatYouGet: Repo mapping prompt for Codex, Cursor, Claude Code, or another coding agent
SYSTEM_MAP.md with architecture, flows, data, integrations, tests, side effects, and unknowns
BUILDPRINT_CANDIDATES.md with scoped reusable Buildprint options
A selected-buildprint/ executable packet with BUILDPRINT.md, 01-questions.md, 02-project-setup.md, blueprint.yaml, phase-index.yaml, phase-flow.md, phase files, 04-evaluation.md, and evidence schema
Phase-flow execution contract requiring .buildprint/phase-runs/<phase-id>/plan.md, team.md, bounded handoffs, returns, reviews, and proof.md
Runtime evidence schema and ledger separation: packaged seed ledger stays immutable; runtime rows go to .buildprint/evidence/evidence-ledger.jsonl
Review contracts for architecture, UX, and QA with concrete evidence instead of ceremonial approval
Fresh-agent replay scoring for read order, review quality, evidence validity, no-fake scans, no parent-context enumeration, and blocker honesty
whatYouNeed: GitHub/private repo access if the source is private
A human scope choice when multiple candidates exist
Permission to use any private source code
includes: Per-phase implementation grammar: ## How to implement this phase
02-project-setup.md architecture, team model, root/local AGENTS plan, quality gates, safety, assumptions, and phase start gate
Bounded specialist loop: product architecture, integration/runtime, persistence, UX/UI, security, test/verification
Proof gates and repair routes per phase
Fresh-agent replay and outcome judge flow
Final chat handover with selected scope, evidence inspected, files generated, commands run, gaps, and next direction
risks: Runtime evidence written into seed ledger
checks: If no target repo URL/path/current repo is clear, the agent asks exactly: Which repo URL or local path should I map?
Discovery happens before extraction unless the user already selected a scope
Generated claims are labeled OBSERVED, INFERRED, QUESTION, or OUT_OF_SCOPE
Secrets and source code are not copied into the generated implementation package

Selected output uses executable-blueprint v5 with BUILDPRINT.md, 01-questions.md, 02-project-setup.md, blueprint.yaml, 03-phases/phase-index.yaml, 03-phases/phase-flow.md, phase files, 04-evaluation.md, and 05-evidence/evidence-ledger.schema.json
Every implementation phase starts with ## How to implement this phase
Agents read phase-flow and evidence-ledger.schema.json before appending runtime evidence
Runtime evidence is appended only to .buildprint/evidence/evidence-ledger.jsonl
Runtime evidence rows include artifact_id, type, phase_id, status, source, proves, proof_type, provider_mode, and upgrades_claim
Blocked, missing, synthetic, partial, sandbox-limited, network-limited, or credential-limited evidence cannot use upgrades_claim:true
no_fake_scan_pass requires a real no-fake scan command or artifact
Architecture, UX, and QA reviews answer required headings with concrete evidence
Fresh-agent replay and outcome judge pass or clearly name remaining gaps

trustBadges.label: Phase-flow replay
trustBadges.detail: Generated packets contain phase-flow.md and per-phase implementation grammar so a fresh agent can execute bounded slices.

trustBadges.label: Evidence honesty
trustBadges.detail: Runtime evidence must be schema-valid; blockers and synthetic checks cannot upgrade production claims.

trustBadges.label: Fresh-agent isolation
trustBadges.detail: Selected packets must be enough for an isolated downstream agent without original source access or parent-directory scans.

trustBadges.label: Review contracts
trustBadges.detail: Architecture, UX, and QA reviews must answer required headings with concrete proof and claim limits.

trustBadges.label: No fake scans
trustBadges.detail: no_fake_scan_pass is accepted only when an actual scan command/artifact exists and ran.

trustBadges.label: MiroFish proof passed
trustBadges.detail: Post-hardening MiroFish map/replay/outcome flow passed with ship-with-notes; replay workspace /tmp/mapper-replay-rDH0JF.

howToUse.title: 1. Point it at a repo
howToUse.detail: Open the source repo in your coding agent, or give the agent a repo URL/local path. If the target is unclear, Mapper OS must ask exactly: Which repo URL or local path should I map?

howToUse.title: 2. Bootstrap the Mapper OS package
howToUse.detail: Copy the agent prompt from this page, or run agb start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/buildprint-mapper-os/package.json and follow .buildprint/next-agent.md. Do not use an agb map command.

howToUse.title: 3. Let it discover, not extract yet
howToUse.detail: The agent should inspect the repo and produce SYSTEM_MAP.md plus BUILDPRINT_CANDIDATES.md. For broad repos, this is where it stops and asks you which candidate to extract.

howToUse.title: 4. Choose the scope
howToUse.detail: Pick one product, feature, workflow, or integration. Smaller is fine; vague whole-repo sludge is not. Full-system mode should be explicit.

howToUse.title: 5. Extract the executable packet
howToUse.detail: The selected package should be an executable-blueprint v5 with BUILDPRINT.md, blueprint.yaml, context/team/UX gates, phase files, evaluation gates, evidence ledger, and a final handover.

resultChecklist: SYSTEM_MAP.md with source-backed architecture, flows, routes/services, data, integrations, side effects, tests, and unknowns
BUILDPRINT_CANDIDATES.md with scoped candidates and clear included/excluded surfaces
A selected-buildprint/ executable packet, not legacy START_HERE/PRE_IMPLEMENTATION_QUESTIONS/03-capabilities routing
01-questions.md with only blocking questions and AI-best-judgment defaults
02-project-setup.md with architecture, team operating model, execution authority, AGENTS plan, quality gates, safety/permissions, assumptions, and phase start gate
03-phases/phase-flow.md plus phase files that start with ## How to implement this phase
Runtime proof plan that writes only to .buildprint/evidence/evidence-ledger.jsonl
Architecture/UX/QA review contracts and repair routing
Final handover with selected scope, evidence inspected, files generated, commands run, known gaps, and recommended next direction
copyPrompt: Use the Buildprint Mapper OS Buildprint. First bootstrap exact snapshots: agb start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/buildprint-mapper-os/package.json . If agb is not installed, clone https://github.com/DomEscobar/agent-buildprint and run node agent-buildprint/bin/agb.js start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/buildprint-mapper-os/package.json . Then read .buildprint/next-agent.md and continue. Do not write Buildprint snapshots manually. Use it to map a target repo in the current agent session. First resolve the target: if the user has not clearly provided a repository URL, local path, or already-opened current working repo, ask exactly one blocking question: 'Which repo URL or local path should I map?' Do not start discovery until the target is explicit. Do not run agb map; that CLI does not exist. After selection, extract only that scope into selected-buildprint/ as executable-blueprint v5 with BUILDPRINT.md, 01-questions.md, 02-project-setup.md, blueprint.yaml, 03-phases/phase-index.yaml, 03-phases/phase-flow.md, phase Markdown files, 04-evaluation.md, and 05-evidence/evidence-ledger.jsonl. BUILDPRINT.md is the only start point. Do not emit START_HERE.md, PRE_IMPLEMENTATION_QUESTIONS.md, 03-capabilities/, 04-interfaces/, 05-state-runtime/, 06-safety/, 08-evaluation/, 09-evidence/, or packet AGENTS.md. 01-questions.md must use numbered questions with AI-best-judgment defaults. 02-project-setup.md must define architecture, team operating model, execution authority through 03-phases/phase-flow.md, delegation/handoff protocol, root/local AGENTS.md plan, quality gates, safety/permissions, assumptions, and the phase start gate. Each implementation phase must start with ## How to implement this phase, require reading 03-phases/phase-flow.md, .buildprint/next-agent.md, and current project AGENTS.md, and remain a proof-gated vertical product slice with interfaces touched, state/runtime touched, UX/UI requirements, safety/security constraints, proof gate, implementation loop, and repair routing. Runtime evidence belongs only in .buildprint/evidence/evidence-ledger.jsonl after phase-flow artifacts exist; packaged 05-evidence/evidence-ledger.jsonl is seed-only. The selected packet must be source-independent enough for a fresh coding agent to implement without original source access. Default to high-quality best-fit implementation, but ask for irreversible, expensive, credentialed, destructive, or product-defining forks. Do not copy secrets, do not invent validation results, and do not flatten the repo into one vague document. Finish with a chat handover containing outcome, selected scope, evidence inspected, files generated, commands run, known gaps, and recommended next direction.
